old: 她对着镜子梳头。
new: 她对着镜子看自己。

old: She combs her hair in front of the mirror.
new: She looks at herself in the mirror.

old: 同学们举他当班长。
new: 同学们举他当队长。
reason: 班长 not in vocab_upto_L4; 队长 is.

old: Classmates nominated him as class monitor.
new: Classmates nominated him as team captain.

old: 委员会拒绝了申请。
new: 学校拒绝了他的申请。

old: The committee rejected the application.
new: The school rejected his application.

old: 咳嗽的时候应该捂住嘴。
new: 咳嗽的时候要用手挡住嘴。
reason: 捂 not in vocab_upto_L4; 挡 also not in vocab — use in-vocab alternative.

old: 咳嗽的时候应该捂住嘴。
new: 咳嗽的时候，不要对着人。
reason: 捂 not in vocab_upto_L4; rewritten to avoid it entirely.

old: You should cover your mouth when coughing.
new: When coughing, don't face people.

old: 登山是一件困难的事。
new: 游泳对他来说是一件困难的事。
reason: 登山 uses 登山 compound not in vocab (登机 is in vocab but not 登山).

old: Climbing a mountain is a challenging thing.
new: Swimming is a challenging thing for him.

old: 他从小就有远大的理想。
new: 他从小就有很大的理想。
reason: 远大 not in vocab_upto_L4 as a compound adjective.

old: He has had great ideals since childhood.
new: He has had big ideals since childhood.

old: 他亮出了底牌。
new: 他亮出了自己的证件。
reason: 底牌 not in vocab_upto_L4; also the idiom is too advanced for L4.

old: He revealed his cards.
new: He revealed his ID.

old: 我没有零钱，只有大钱。
new: 我没有零钱，只有一百元。
reason: 大钱 (big bills) not in vocab_upto_L4 as a compound.

old: I don't have change — only big bills.
new: I don't have change — only a hundred yuan.

old: 另外，你需要带护照。
new: 另外一本书在桌子上。
reason: Sentence illustrates 'in addition' (conjunction usage) not 'other' (the given definition for this row); fix to show pronoun/determiner use of 另外 meaning 'other/another'.

old: In addition, you need to bring your passport.
new: The other book is on the table.

old: 短视频现在很流行。
new: 这种游戏现在很流行。

old: Short videos are very popular now.
new: This type of game is very popular now.

old: 宽松的衣服现在很流行。
new: 红色的衣服现在很流行。

old: Loose-fitting clothes are very in vogue now.
new: Red clothes are very in vogue now.

old: 这款手机非常流行。
new: 这种手机非常流行。
reason: 款 as a measure word for phone models not in vocab_upto_L4; 种 is.

old: This phone model is very in vogue.
new: This type of phone is very in vogue.
